title: First Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Concludes, Mediators Say
summary: Qatari and Pakistani officials said there was “encouraging progress” toward a lasting peace deal during talks in Switzerland. But strains over Lebanon were apparent, and President Trump renewed threats against Iran.

title: U.K. Live Updates: Starmer Announces Resignation; Burnham Wins Key Endorsement
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer stepped down as leader of the governing Labour Party. Andy Burnham, the party’s most popular politician, said he would seek the prime minister’s job and secured the support of a potential rival.

title: Who Is Andy Burnham, the Man Who Could Be Britain’s Next Prime Minister?
summary: Charismatic, northern and exuding a relaxed optimism, Mr. Burnham is a contrast to Keir Starmer. His allies hope he could mend Labour’s relationship with voters.

title: The Key Players in the U.S.-Iran Talks, and the Deadly Rise of Giant Trucks and S.U.V.s
First Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Ends With High Hopes and Big Challenges
summary: Mediators reported progress toward reaching a final deal within 60 days. They also said that negotiators had dwelled on issues that were supposed to be settled.

title: Oil Prices Fall as U.S.-Iran Talks Show Signs of Progress
summary: Oil prices retreated after Iran’s foreign minister said there had been “major progress” toward ending the fighting in Lebanon during the first session of high-level talks.

title: Going to a National Park This Summer? Here’s What to Expect.
summary: Huge visitor numbers, sharply reduced staffing, scrapped reservation systems and higher entry fees for nonresidents could make your trip a bit less serene.

title: Embattled Superintendent of Los Angeles School District Resigns
summary: Alberto Carvalho resigned Sunday, months after the F.B.I. raided his home and office. He came to Los Angeles from Miami with a reputation for raising student test scores but also made missteps.

title: In Utah House Race, Progressives Assail Democrat Who Backed Abortion Restrictions
summary: Ben McAdams, a moderate former congressman, faces a handful of left-wing opponents in a rare opportunity for a Democrat to win in a Republican state.

title: For Schlossberg, Quirky Charm and a Claim to Camelot May Not Be Enough
summary: Jack Schlossberg, the grandson of John F. Kennedy, has found it difficult to overcome some of his perceived shortcomings in a high-profile House primary race.

title: Thousands Are Fleeing Ukraine’s Donbas Strongholds as Russia Pushes Closer
summary: While Kyiv’s fortunes have brightened in other ways in the war, Moscow’s forces are raining bombs and drones on “fortress belt” cities like Sloviansk and Kramatorsk.

title: China Tightens Rare Earth Grip on U.S. Firms, Threatening Trade Clash
summary: The move targets two U.S. manufacturers at the center of the Trump administration’s effort to rebuild the domestic supply chain for critical magnets.

title: A Loophole Brings Cystic Fibrosis Patients a ‘Miracle Drug’ in Generic Form
summary: A generic version of a breakthrough cystic fibrosis drug, manufactured in Bangladesh for a fraction of the American price, may give some families around the world an unlikely lifeline.

title: Cuba’s Fuel Crisis Brings Schools to a Standstill
summary: Cuba’s already struggling schools are ending the academic year early because of a crippling fuel shortage caused by the U.S. oil blockade.

title: Could I Become a Kickboxer at 48? I Went to Thailand to Find Out.
summary: A week inside a grueling Muay Thai training program in Phuket.

title: Ten Years After Brexit, the Dismal Verdict Is In
summary: Brexit was supposed to let Britain return to a time when it still counted as a global power. A decade later, the costs are blindingly apparent.

title: The Real Reason Bosses Want You Back in the Office Full Time (It’s Not Productivity)
Behind the Wheel
summary: We look into the dangers posed by large S.U.V.s and pickup trucks.

title: California Needs Water and Clean Power. It Might Have a Fix for Both.
summary: A pilot program is building solar panels over irrigation canals to generate electricity. As a bonus, the shade prevents water from evaporating.
